summary: The Goshen Township Board of Zoning Appeals held a public hearing on case BZA 2022-024, a variance request by Troy E. Smith to place an accessory structure (personal garage) in the front yard of a corner lot in the R3 zoning district. Staff explained the property's corner lot designation creates two front yards, requiring a 50-foot setback from the Ethel Lynn right-of-way. The board discussed topography concerns and ultimately approved a 32-foot variance allowing the structure to be aligned with an existing gazebo, 18 feet from the property line. The applicant was not present at the meeting.
decisions: Approved video meeting minutes from previous meeting

Approved a 32-foot variance on BZA 2022-024 for Troy E. Smith, allowing the proposed accessory structure to be aligned with the existing gazebo at 18 feet from the Ethel Lynn right-of-way property line
Motion to close the Board of Zoning Appeals meeting approved
action_items: Hobart to send out meeting calendars once finalized
Troy E. Smith to comply with the approved variance conditions, aligning the proposed structure with the gazebo
key_amounts: 50-foot front yard setback required by zoning code
32-foot variance granted (from 50-foot requirement to 18 feet from property line)
18 feet from property line to gazebo (reference point for approved variance)
25 feet from edge of gravel pavement to property line
10-foot distance proposed by applicant from county right-of-way to structure
16 by 18 foot existing shed to be removed
notable_topics: Variance request for accessory structure placement in front yard of corner lot (BZA 2022-024)
Corner lot definition and dual front yard requirements
Ethel Lynn road status - dedicated county right-of-way but not built to county standards and unmaintained
Topography concerns with natural drainage on the lot affecting structure placement
Christman Subdivision plat dedication of Plum Street, Wood Street, and Ethel Lynn
Discussion of paper street concept and its implications for nonconforming properties
notes: The applicant Troy E. Smith was not present at the hearing. The board discussed the unusual situation of Ethel Lynn being a dedicated county right-of-way that was never built to county standards and remains a gravel road not maintained by either the township or county. The board referenced consulting with the county deputy surveyor and county prosecutor's office regarding the road status and corner lot designation. Next BZA meeting scheduled for January 23rd, maintaining the fourth Monday schedule.
